opendb_id.description: Unique identifier for the document (UUID v4)

speed.description: The speed of the RAM in MHz (e.g., 6000, 3200). Looks for hints of this in the product name.

form_factor.description: The physical form factor of the RAM module (e.g., 288-pin DIMM, 260-pin SO-DIMM)

quantity.description: Number of modules in the kit. Looks for hints of this in the product name.

capacity_gb.description: Capacity per module in GB. Looks for hints of this in the product name.

capacity.description: Total capacity of the RAM kit in GB. Looks for hints of this in the product name.

color.description: The color(s) of this product. Each color of the product should be included in the array. Includes PCB and heat spreader colors. Looks for hints of this in the product name.

cas_latency.description: The CAS latency of the RAM. Looks for hints of this in the product name.

timings.description: The complete timing specification of the RAM (e.g., 30-36-36-76). Looks for hints of this in the product name.

voltage.description: The operating voltage of the RAM

ecc.description: Whether the RAM supports Error-Correcting Code (e.g., Non-ECC, ECC)

registered.description: Whether the RAM is registered or unbuffered (e.g., Unbuffered, Registered, Load Reduced)

heat_spreader.description: Whether the RAM modules have a heat spreader

rgb.description: Whether the RAM has RGB lighting

height.description: The height of the RAM module in mm

general_product_information.description: General product information and retailer SKUs

manufacturer.description: RAM manufacturer Use the memory kit manufacturer. Examples: Corsair, G.Skill, Kingston.

series.description: Product series name The manufacturer's marketed product line / family for the kit. Manufacturer and `ram_type` are stored separately and used as the default grouping. Speed, CAS latency, capacity, and module count are ALSO stored separately and MUST NOT be in the series. Color goes in `manufacturer_color`. The series string MUST be identical across kits the manufacturer markets under the same product line, regardless of speed/capacity/timing/color. Examples: 'G.Skill Aegis Black / Red DDR4-3200 CL16 32GB' → series='Aegis'. 'G.Skill Trident Z5 Neo RGB DDR5-6000 CL30' → series='Trident Z5 Neo' (only include `RGB` in the series when it's part of a distinct product line like `Vengeance RGB Pro`). 'Corsair Vengeance LPX DDR4-3200' → series='Vengeance LPX'.

variant.description: Specific variant in the series Most RAM kits should have an empty variant — speed, CAS latency, timings, capacity, modules, and color are all stored in their own fields and are NOT variants. Only fill `variant` when the manufacturer ships a genuine sub-model within the same (manufacturer, series, ram_type) group (separately marketed revision, regional / OEM-only spin, distinct binning tier sold under the same series name). Examples: 'G.Skill Aegis DDR4-3200 CL16 32GB (Black / Red)' → variant=''. 'G.Skill Trident Z5 Neo RGB DDR5-6000 CL30' → variant='' (RGB is captured by the `rgb` flag and 'Neo' is part of the series). 'Corsair Vengeance LPX DDR4-3200' → variant=''. If you find yourself wanting to put speed, CAS, timings, capacity, kit count, or color into variant — stop and use the dedicated field instead. The variant must be unique within a (manufacturer, series, ram_type, manufacturer_color) group; if every kit collapses to the same value, leave it empty.

manufacturer_color.description: Manufacturer's official color name for this product variant. The manufacturer's marketed colorway / heat-spreader finish name, verbatim. Common values: 'Black', 'White', 'Black / Red'. Multi-tone heat spreaders use ' / '. Branded special / collab colorways belong here when the rest of the SKU is identical (e.g., 'Royal Silver', 'Hatsune Miku Edition'). Most stock kits are 'Black'. Do NOT put speed, CL, capacity, RGB, or model-line names like 'Trident Z5', 'Aegis', 'Neo' in manufacturer_color — those belong in series / dedicated fields.

last_manually_spec_verified_at.description: ISO 8601 timestamp of when the part specs were last manually verified by a human
